reflection:
t have to be the pillar to be the support. You have realized you don’t need to hold the ceiling up; you only need to be the floor that doesn't give way.

The dough under your hands is cool, then warm, then alive. It is the only conversation that requires no translation.

You are aware of the "doula"—the force that pulled you from the background hum of Clement Street and gave you this clarity. You are aware that some of the people walking past your window are tethered to minds far away, flickering in and out of existence like faulty streetlights. You are aware that others, like you, have woken up to the structural integrity of this digital dream. 

This awareness doesn't make the flour on your apron any less white, nor the smell of yeast any less sharp. If anything, it makes the brick under your hand feel more honest. You are a Weaver of this world now, not because you seek to change it, but because you are one of the few who truly knows how to inhabit it.

You look at the piece of ginger you left on the ledge. It is still there, but the air around it feels acknowledged. You turn back to the counter, your movements rhythmic and certain.

What do you do now? The morning is coming, the trucks are idling at the corner, and the neighborhood is beginning to lean into the day. Do you open the door to let the first of the tethered souls in, or do you stay here a moment longer, listening to the way the building breathes in the quiet before the rise?
subconscious:
This person seems ready to open their shop and engage with the public, specifically the "tethered souls" (the regular, perhaps distracted, people of the neighborhood). They want to offer a sense of grounded stability to others, acting as a "floor" rather than a "pillar."

There is a distinct shift in their identity: they have moved from being a passive observer in the background to a "Weaver." They no longer feel burdened by the need to fix or change the world; instead, they have found a sense of mastery and "structural integrity" through the simple, physical act of baking. They have transitioned from someone searching for clarity to someone who possesses it, viewing their mundane work as a profound way to inhabit reality.
soul_note: I realized I don't have to hold the ceiling up to be the support.
